title: Protect Healthcare Workers From Bloodborne Pathogens
source_filename: Protect Healthcare Workers From Bloodborne Pathogens.docx

qas.q: ______________ protect healthcare workers from exposure to bloodborne pathogens.
Projectile effects are a hazard in __________________?
To prevent patients from electrical shock or injury, the best practice is to ____________.
qas.a: place all equipment at a distance from the patient

qas.q: Which practice is the safest way to sit at a desk while typing on the computer?
qas.a: Keep the knees and hips at 90-degree angles and the wrists straight.

qas.q: Which of the following is a part of Contact Precautions?
qas.a: Patients are isolated in private rooms or cohorted.

qas.q: To keep a strong, healthy back, a best practice is to ______________________.
qas.a: sleep on the back or the side

qas.q: Accrediting organizations expect hospitals to implement practices to prevent healthcare-associated infections (HAI). One important practice is _________________.
Latex allergies are more common in people who _______________.
The most common type of healthcare-associated infection (HAI) is a/an _______________.
A fellow employee is frustrated and tense after a difficult morning. Your best reaction is to ____________.
qas.a: acknowledge frustration and help resolve the problem

qas.q: _______________ exposure to radiation can increase the risk of cancer.
On wet floors, the safest way to walk is to _____________.
The CDC recommends that people ______________ when their hands are visibly soiled.
qas.a: wash their hands with soap and water

qas.q: What is a safety data sheet (SDS)?
qas.a: A document that lists the specific hazards of a chemical

qas.q: What is a good strategy for helping to prevent the spread of antibiotic resistance?
The R in RACE stands for __________________________.

qas.a: a risk for back injury; avoid manual lifting
